insertions.translation_zh: Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements and Significant Accounting Policies
insertions.target_text: Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements and Significant Accounting Policies

insertions.translation_zh:
AIA Holdings Limited (the "Company") was incorporated as a limited liability company in Hong Kong on August 24, 2009. Its registered office address is 1 Connaught Road Central, Central, Hong Kong No. 35th Floor, AIA Financial Center. AIA Holdings Limited is listed on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited (stock code: "1299"); its American Depositary Receipts (Level 1) are traded on the OTC market Make a sale (transaction number is "AAGIY"). AIA Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries (collectively, "AIA" or the "Group") are a financial services provider mainly engaged in the provision of life insurance, with operations throughout Asia Pacific 18 markets in the district. The Group’s principal activities are underwriting life insurance business, providing life, accident and medical insurance and savings plans in Asia, and distributing related Investment and other financial services products.

2. Principal accounting policies 2.1 Preparation of baselines and compliance statements
insertions.target_text:
AIA Holdings Limited (the "Company") was incorporated as a limited liability company in Hong Kong on August 24, 2009. Its registered office address is 1 Connaught Road Central, Central, Hong Kong No. 35th Floor, AIA Financial Center. AIA Holdings Limited is listed on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited (stock code: "1299"); its American Depositary Receipts (Level 1) are traded on the OTC market Make a sale (transaction number is "AAGIY"). AIA Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries (collectively, "AIA" or the "Group") are a financial services provider mainly engaged in the provision of life insurance, with operations throughout Asia Pacific 18 markets in the district. The Group’s principal activities are underwriting life insurance business, providing life, accident and medical insurance and savings plans in Asia, and distributing related Investment and other financial services products.

2. Principal accounting policies 2.1 Preparation of baselines and compliance statements

background_cover.reason: cover multiline source redaction bands with one continuous local background before target text insertion

insertions.translation_zh: The consolidated financial statements have been prepared in accordance with all applicable Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards, International Financial Reporting Standards and the Hong Kong Companies Ordinance. IFRS generally Consistent with Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards, the accounting policies selected by the Group in preparing these consolidated financial statements enable the Group to comply with both Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards and international Financial reporting standards. References in these consolidated financial statements to International Financial Reporting Standards, International Accounting Standards and interpretations developed by the International Financial Reporting Interpretations Committee shall be deemed to refer to the corresponding Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards, Hong Kong Accounting Standards and Hong Kong (International Financial Reporting Interpretations Committee) interpretations, as the case may be. Therefore, there is no There are no differences in accounting practices between HKFRS and IFRS that would affect these consolidated financial statements. The consolidated financial statements were approved for publication by the board of directors on March 12, 2020. The consolidated financial statements have been prepared using the historical cost method and include information on available-for-sale financial assets, certain financial assets and liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss, and derivative financial instruments. Revised for the revaluation of furniture, properties held for own use and investment properties (all carried at fair value). Items included in the consolidated financial statements of each entity in the Group are measured in the currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates (functional currency). The company’s functional currency And the presentation currency of the Company and the Group is US dollars. The consolidated financial statements are presented in millions of U.S. dollars unless otherwise stated. Except as described below, the accounting policies adopted are consistent with those adopted in prior financial periods.

The company’s functional currency And the presentation currency of the Company and the Group is US dollars. The consolidated financial statements are presented in millions of U.S. dollars unless otherwise stated. Except as described below, the accounting policies adopted are consistent with those adopted in prior financial periods.
